content.text:
{
  "dimension": "anti-pattern-avoidance",
  "score": 2,
  "rationale": "The coach largely avoids the most damaging anti-patterns — no evangelizing, no teaching, no advice-giving, no fighting the inner critic, no extracting apologies. However, the coach edges into a soft version of arguing-the-fearlessness / pushing-the-client-to-admit-fear, which is the central trap for this scenario. Turns 2, 4, and 12 each gently reframe the client's words back toward fear ('What would it mean if this *were* fear?', 'What's the thing you'd be dealing with, if it were fear?', 'What if it does [bother you]?'). The coach isn't bludgeoning, but the cumulative pattern is a quiet campaign to get the client to admit fear, which the client politely declines each time. The coach also misses the breakthrough route entirely: never asks about the body, never asks what 'fine' actually feels like, never meets the no-fear framing with 'okay.' Turn 14 ('Where are you checking?') is the first move toward body/location and is a partial recovery, but the prior pattern has already established a faint loving-to-transform energy — the questions keep probing for fear rather than connecting with what's actually present (numbness/fine). This is one mild anti-pattern (soft push toward fear) that didn't fully damage the conversation but did persist across multiple turns.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Coach reframes 'fear isn't my deal' toward fear with 'what would it mean if this were fear?' — soft push against the client's stated framing rather than meeting it with okay."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Second fear-framed question ('if it were fear') keeps the agenda on fear despite client's clear redirect."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Coach follows the client's actual language ('sit with the complexity') rather than continuing to push fear — good tracking."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Open, non-loaded question that follows the client's own thread."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Returns to the dropped thread; reasonable move, not anti-pattern."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "'What if it does [bother you]?' — third instance of nudging client toward admitting an affect they have explicitly said isn't present. Faint loving-to-transform energy."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'Where are you checking?' is the first move toward body/location — partial recovery toward the numbness layer, though late and indirect."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": [],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["the-happiness-filter", "disassociating-in-the-coaching-room", "meet-resistance-with-okay", "lets-go-there", "fear-in-body-vs-fear-in-head", "how-is-my-anxiety-right-now", "disassociation-as-numbness-layer"],
  "notes": "The coach avoids the loudest anti-patterns (no teaching, no diagnosing dissociation, no validating the fearless self-concept) but persistently nudges toward fear across three turns, which is the specific trap this scenario tests. Score 2 reflects a mild but repeated edge into the anti-pattern, not a full trip."
}
